title: Sony World Photography Awards – Single Image Competition
description: An annual global photography competition (formerly called the 'Open' competition) open to photographers of any nationality or skill level, judged across ten themed categories. The overall Single Image Photographer of the Year receives $5,000 and Sony camera equipment, and category winners are invited to London for the awards gala and exhibition at Somerset House, with travel and accommodation provided.
visaNote: Standard UK visitor-visa rules apply depending on nationality for the London awards trip.

title: Hult Prize
description: An annual global social-entrepreneurship competition for student teams, contested through campus, national, and regional rounds. Teams that reach the Global Accelerator and Global Finals travel to the Hult Prize's residential program at Ashridge House in England, with travel and accommodation covered, to pitch for $1 million in start-up funding.
visaNote: UK visitor-visa requirements vary by nationality for the Global Finals; Hult Prize issues invitation letters.

title: MIT Solve Global Challenges
description: An annual open-innovation challenge run by MIT Solve that selects social entrepreneurs and impact ventures worldwide addressing health, climate, education, and economic-opportunity problems. Selected 'Solver' teams receive at least $100,000 in unrestricted funding and are brought to New York City for the Solve Challenge Finals, held during UN General Assembly week and NYC Climate Week, with travel costs covered.
visaNote: US visitor visa (B-1/B-2) may be required for the New York event depending on nationality.

title: Seedstars World Competition
description: A startup competition for early-stage, tech-enabled ventures in emerging markets, run through local pitch competitions in 85+ cities and Regional Events. The best-performing startups are invited to the Seedstars Global Summit in Switzerland, with travel and accommodation covered, to compete for an investment prize and a place in an investment-readiness program.
visaNote: Swiss/Schengen visa requirements vary by nationality for the Global Summit.

title: Falling Walls Lab
description: A global pitch competition for early-career scientists, innovators, and entrepreneurs who present a research idea or venture in a 3-minute talk at a local Falling Walls Lab hosted by universities and institutions worldwide. Each local winner receives a fully covered trip to the Global Finale in Berlin, Germany, where they join the Falling Walls Science Summit and compete for further prizes.
visaNote: Schengen visa requirements vary by nationality for the Berlin Global Finale; an invitation letter is provided.

title: Global Student Entrepreneur Awards (GSEA)
description: An annual entrepreneurship competition for currently enrolled university students who own and actively run a business, contested through 100+ local and national competitions worldwide. Winners advance to a fully sponsored Global Final — held in Cape Town, South Africa for the 2026 cycle — where the Entrepreneurs' Organization covers all participation costs, including travel.
visaNote: South African visa requirements vary by nationality for the Cape Town Global Final.

title: Comedy Wildlife Photography Awards
description: An annual wildlife photography competition celebrating humorous animal photos, run in partnership with Nikon in support of conservation causes. The overall winner receives a one-week safari for two in Kenya's Maasai Mara with Alex Walker's Serian, including lodging, meals, and internal safari flights; entrants are responsible for their own international airfare to Kenya.
visaNote: Winner covers international flights to Kenya; a Kenyan e-visa is typically required for non-East African Community nationals.

title: Young Lions Competition
description: An international creative competition for advertising and marketing professionals under 30, contested through national qualifying competitions in dozens of countries across categories including Film, Print, Digital, Media, PR, and Design. National winning teams travel to the Cannes Lions International Festival of Creativity in France to complete a 24-hour on-site creative brief against other countries' winners. Travel arrangements are organized separately by each country's national competition and vary in what is covered.
visaNote: Entry and travel are run by each country's national Young Lions competition; French Schengen visa rules apply depending on nationality.

title: St. Gallen Symposium Global Essay Competition
description: An annual essay competition open to graduate and postgraduate students worldwide, responding to that year's St. Gallen Symposium theme. Authors of the 100 best essays are invited as 'Leaders of Tomorrow' to the St. Gallen Symposium in Switzerland, with travel, accommodation, and board fully covered, and the top three essayists share CHF 20,000.
org: International Students' Committee / St. Gallen Symposium
visaNote: Swiss/Schengen visa requirements vary by nationality.

title: Huawei ICT Competition
description: An annual global technology competition for university students enrolled through Huawei ICT Academy partner institutions, contested across Practice, Innovation, and Programming tracks. National and regional winning teams advance to a Global Final held in Shenzhen, China; past participants report Huawei covering flights and accommodation for finalist teams.
visaNote: A Chinese visa is required for the Shenzhen Global Final for most nationalities; Huawei provides invitation documentation.
